title: Console frontend MQTT-over-WSS realtime with SSE fallback
description:
## Intent
Use browser MQTT-over-WSS as primary realtime path while preserving SSE fallback.

## Scope
- Realtime bootstrap API consumption
- IoT MQTT client integration in console frontend
- Fallback logic to SSE on MQTT failure

## Definition of Done
- Browser receives tenant events via IoT MQTT.
- Fallback path activates cleanly when MQTT fails.
- No route regression for `/:account/:project`.
- Client behavior and failure modes are documented.
creator: ryan.porter

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: Started frontend realtime integration work.
Evidence: task moved open -> in_progress; added client bootstrap fetch for /api/realtime/bootstrap and route-scoped logging in App.tsx.
Next: wire actual MQTT-over-WSS client subscription path using bootstrap payload and keep SSE as fallback.
Risk/Blocker: none.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: Added frontend bootstrap contract consumption for cloud realtime settings.
Evidence: `apps/console/src/api/client.ts` now exposes `fetchRealtimeBootstrap`; `App.tsx` fetches and logs route-scoped bootstrap metadata; `npm run typecheck` passes.
Next: implement MQTT-over-WSS client transport and bind live events to issue state updates with SSE fallback retained.
Risk/Blocker: none.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: Added realtime transport orchestration in console client (MQTT-over-WSS attempt first, SSE fallback on bootstrap/connect/subscribe failure).
Evidence: apps/console/src/api/client.ts now exports subscribeToRealtimeFeed; added mqtt dependency and typecheck pass.
Next: validate live IoT WSS auth path with Cognito credentials in deployed env and close after browser E2E proof.
Risk/Blocker: live MQTT subscription verification requires deployed stack + credentials; currently gated by deploy blocker in tskl-eo2.5.1.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: Frontend realtime task now expands to include cloud login UX and authenticated request flow before MQTT fallback validation.
Evidence: current app shell/API behavior verified by curl; no browser redirect/login flow exists yet for unauth users.
Next: add Hosted UI PKCE adapter, auth bootstrap contract, and route guard behavior while preserving local-mode no-auth flow.
Risk/Blocker: none for implementation; webhook secret IAM blocker remains for final E2E signoff.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: Folded auth-bootstrap + bearer-aware data/SSE client behavior into frontend realtime path based on deployed smoke feedback.
Evidence: App boot now calls auth bootstrap and installs auth header/query providers before snapshot/realtime subscription; npm typecheck/build and cargo test passing.
Next: browser verify Hosted UI redirect/callback plus MQTT primary->SSE fallback behavior in cloud env.
Risk/Blocker: signed webhook evidence remains blocked by Secrets Manager IAM gap (tracked in 5.4/5.7).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: Frontend realtime/auth integration pass landed with commit-backed evidence.
Evidence: commit 385d623; authenticated fetch/SSE wiring and auth-bootstrap gate in App.tsx/client.ts; build/typecheck/test suites pass.
Next: browser E2E for Hosted UI + MQTT primary with SSE fallback forced-failure path.
Risk/Blocker: signed webhook launch evidence still blocked by Secrets Manager IAM gap.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: Browser MQTT-over-WSS primary path is now live in deployed cloud and validated in full pre-acceptance run.
Evidence: artifacts/cloud-validation/20260307T222139Z/gate6-browser_probe.log includes [realtime] mqtt subscribed and [notifications] received.
Next: keep SSE fallback as compatibility path and move to UX polish/review.
Risk/Blocker: none for MQTT primary functionality.
